Agreed; expanding further, it’s important to note that while Bacteroides spp., particularly *B. fragilis*, play significant roles not only as facultative anaerobes taking advantage of microaerophilic niches created by compromised tissue integrity but also possess direct virulence attributes enhancing pathogenicity in deeper soft-tissue spaces crucial for head-and-neck infections. These include production of toxins and enzymes facilitating invasion and evasion strategies against host defenses. Thus, confirming once more:
